question: Is claude-fable-5 worth $10/$50 per MTok for any extraction metric?
config: Pass1+2 = claude-fable-5 via prompted-JSON adapter (constrained decoding rejected: schema >16 nullable unions), Pass3 = gemini-3-flash-preview (production default)

description: claude_structured() adapter: model dispatch in gemini_structured() on claude- prefix; prompted-JSON with schema embedded in prompt; adaptive thinking; streaming; usage/cost accounting printed per run
rationale: No Anthropic structured-output path existed. output_config.format failed twice: subset-required objects -> grammar compilation timeout; require-all+nullable -> 23 union params > 16 limit.

variance_note: fable5 agg spread 0.715-0.729 across 3 runs vs prod 0.639-0.670

patterns.name: wrong_anchor_dates
patterns.detail: AI infers anchor year (e.g. death 2012 vs GT 2016); all dependent shifts miss the 270-day window. dateCertainty=unknown would match.

patterns.name: saturation_violations
patterns.detail: one functional pattern coded as 2-3 events (caregiving: took-on + took-leave + called-crying)
patterns.risk: consolidation rules historically kill TP on Gemini

patterns.name: married_person_spouse_asymmetry
patterns.detail: match_events requires ai_person==gt_person AND ai_spouse==gt_spouse; AI picking the other partner can never match

change_description: PASS1: emit at most ONE birth event per person (dedup rule)
reason: Anthropic API credit balance exhausted mid-run; edit applied to worktree prompts but untested
ANTHROPIC_EXTRACTION_API_KEY account out of credits after ~$20 (3 baseline + 1 diagnostic run)

change_description: PASS1: emit at most ONE birth event per person
rationale: Removed disc-48 duplicate birth (AI 26->25, disc F1 +0.013); discs 37/39 dupes are conflicting-age dupes the rule doesn't cover; overall delta within single-run noise; no disc-level regression. Note: discs 36/37/39 outputs byte-identical to diag run - Fable 5 near-deterministic per-discussion.

change_description: PASS2: dateCertainty=unknown for purely-inferred dates (opens F1 date gate)
rationale: Events -0.016 vs baseline mean exceeds 0.01 revert threshold. Disc 48 gained 1 TP as intended but over-extraction rose elsewhere (disc 51 FP +3, disc 50 FP +2). Also integrity risk: unknown certainty fully opens date gate -> potential false TPs.

change_description: PASS2: concrete saturation example (caregiving trio = ONE overfunctioning event; distinct symptoms stay separate)
rationale: Disc 37 +1 TP (0.453->0.491), no disc-level regressions, best single run of induction.

change_description: PASS1: extend birth dedup to conflicting ages/dates + pre-return self-check

f1_scores.note: 5-discussion average - disc 51 failed on API credits, NOT comparable to 6-disc baselines

rationale: Run contaminated by billing failure on disc 51. 5-disc comparison vs iter3 first-5 inconclusive (0.557 vs 0.570, disc 36 FP +2, disc 39 FP -1). Edit retained in worktree pending a clean run; low-risk rule extension of kept iter1.

reason: Three clean iterations all within single-run noise of cold baseline (0.721/0.592). Prompts are already heavily tuned; Fable 5 gains come from the model, not induction. Iteration 4 untestable (credits).

blockers.item: Anthropic constrained decoding for PDPDeltas (>16 nullable unions; grammar compile timeout). Prompted-JSON adapter is the workaround; 0 parse failures in ~70 calls.
married-event person/spouse slot asymmetry in match_events - AI picking the other partner can never match; symmetric canonicalization would fix the metric
GT placeholder events ('New Event', person=None births) and GT statement-date anchoring convention vs AI historical-date anchoring
Residual shift-event over-extraction (FP ~11-16/disc): prompt consolidation historically kills TP proportionally; would need a review/consensus pass, not prompt edits

reason: API credits exhausted again mid-iter4 (~$20 spent this session). Phase 5 per-pass hybrid analysis unfunded - the cheapest decision-relevant config (flash-lite P1+P2 + Fable Pass 3) costs ~$1.80/run.

change_description: VERIFICATION RERUN (clean, 6 discs): conflicting-age birth dedup + self-check
rationale: No regression vs cold baseline; dedup rules are free hygiene. Disc 36 Events 0.813 (record single-disc).

insight: The two levers are independent: Fable on extraction buys Events (+0.165); Fable on Pass-3 SARF review buys SARF macro (+0.16 to +0.25) at ~$0.20/discussion. SARF S and F cross Stage 4 (>0.5) in both Fable-P3 configs.

decision: EXCLUDE kept prompt edits (birth dedup, saturation example) from PR - no measured benefit on production Gemini, weak-negative at N=2, per asymmetric-risk lesson. Edits remain experimental in fable-5-extraction worktree.
